_comment: EXAMPLE PACK semantic-layer config (OPTIONAL — requires sentence-transformers; ask the journalist before building, it downloads a ~90MB model and takes minutes to an hour depending on corpus size). Spaces = embedding indexes over text columns; bridge = the anchored meaning-level coupling detector.
_model_revision_note: pin to a HuggingFace commit hash for reproducible rebuilds
_comment_chunking: 170 words keeps whole chunks inside MiniLM's 256-token window

spaces.name: grant_narratives
spaces.where: description IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(description) > 20

bridge._comment: The anchored coupling detector. Candidate pairs come ONLY from documented relationships (anchor SQLs below); embeddings rank within them. Unanchored corpus-wide topic matching produces topical coincidences, not leads — do not remove the anchor requirement.

anchors.name: shared_vendor_ref
anchors.sql: SELECT DISTINCT g.recipient_name, o.officer_name FROM norm_grants g JOIN norm_vendor_officers o ON o.vendor_ref = g.vendor_ref

specificity_filter._comment: drop match-everything boilerplate unless it carries a distinctive token

innocent_explanations: Similar language often means similar lawful work, not similar misconduct
A documented anchor is a relationship, not evidence of causation
Semantic similarity is model-derived; the lead's authority is the two verbatim texts, never the score
